summary: Added the direct capture helper build preflight to hosted Linux CI so the Wayland/GBM/libdrm build surface is checked by the Linux Nix validation path without making macOS CI responsible for Linux-only dependencies.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux CI runs `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight` or an equivalent Linux-only gate.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight` in the Linux job after `nix flake check`.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS CI is not made responsible for Linux/Nix direct-capture dependencies.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml leaves the macOS job unchanged; docs/DIRECT_CAPTURE_HELPER_PREFLIGHT.md records that macOS CI does not run this preflight.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence records local `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight`, `nix flake check`, and `nix develop -c just check`.
No runtime capture, product QUIC, portal, capture-to-NVENC, zero-copy, cross-device, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/DIRECT_CAPTURE_HELPER_PREFLIGHT.md and evidence/m4-direct-capture-preflight-ci-gate/notes.md state the build-only boundary and excluded claims.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node is a hosted Linux CI build/preflight gate. Runtime capture and hardware/media-path behavior are out of scope.
